skill.name: meta-criteria-generator
skill.description: Generates scientifically sound inclusion and exclusion criteria for Meta-Analysis based on a given title or keywords. Use when user wants to design eligibility criteria for a systematic review or meta-analysis.
skill.summary: Generates scientifically sound inclusion and exclusion criteria for Meta-Analysis based on a given title or keywords using the PICO framework.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 42 static analysis findings evaluated as false positives. The 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings matched documentation text in SKILL.md and script argument parser lines, not actual crypto code. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings matched markdown code block examples, not executable code. The only confirmed risk is external_commands: the skill documents Python script execution, which is legitimate for its text extraction purpose.

low_findings.title: Dismissed: Weak cryptographic algorithm
low_findings.description: 16 findings flagged as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' across SKILL.md, scripts/extract_criteria.py, scripts/validate_skill.py, and the audit result JSON. All are false positives. The scanner matched words like 'key' in documentation context (e.g., 'Key Features' at SKILL.md:17, 'keywords' at SKILL.md:3). No cryptographic algorithms exist in any skill file. The scripts contain only argparse setup and regex text extraction.

low_findings.title: Dismissed: System reconnaissance
low_findings.description: 3 findings flagged as 'System reconnaissance'. At SKILL.md:48 the phrase 'Output discipline: keep results reproducible' is documentation guidance, not reconnaissance. At audit result JSON lines 225 and 260 the text contains PASS status notes for assertions. No system enumeration or data gathering behavior exists.

low_findings.title: Dismissed: Hardcoded URL (documentation reference)
low_findings.description: 2 findings flagged as 'Hardcoded URL' at SKILL.md:7. The URL https://github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills is a source attribution link in the documentation header. It is a static reference link, not a network request endpoint. No dynamic network operations exist in this skill.

low_findings.title: Dismissed: Ruby/shell backtick execution (documentation code blocks)
low_findings.description: 24 findings flagged as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across SKILL.md. All matched bash command examples inside markdown code blocks (lines 30-34, 54-56, 92-94, 144-146, 150). These are documentation examples showing how to run the Python scripts. They are not executable code blocks.

content.user_title: Generate Meta-Analysis Inclusion and Exclusion Criteria
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually defining eligibility criteria for systematic reviews and meta-analyses. This skill generates scientifically sound inclusion and exclusion criteria in minutes using the PICO framework.
actual_capabilities: Generate inclusion criteria based on Population, Intervention, Comparator, and Outcomes (PICO) framework
Generate exclusion criteria that are logically consistent with inclusion criteria
Extract and format criteria from LLM text output using the Python extraction script
Structure final output into clearly separated Inclusion and Exclusion sections
Produce reproducible criteria output suitable for systematic review protocols
limitations: Requires user to provide a clear topic or keywords for the meta-analysis
Criteria quality depends on the specificity of the user-provided inputs
Does not perform actual meta-analysis calculations or statistical analysis
Output focuses on eligibility criteria only, not a full systematic review protocol

use_cases.title: Define criteria for a systematic review
use_cases.description: Medical researchers can quickly generate PICO-based inclusion and exclusion criteria when starting a new systematic review or meta-analysis project.
use_cases.target_user: Medical researcher or PhD student planning a systematic review

use_cases.title: Standardize criteria across reviewers
use_cases.description: Research teams can produce consistent eligibility criteria to ensure multiple reviewers apply the same standards when screening studies.
use_cases.target_user: Research team lead coordinating literature review

use_cases.title: Learn systematic review methodology
use_cases.description: Graduate students learning meta-analysis methodology can see how PICO elements translate into structured eligibility criteria.
use_cases.target_user: Graduate student in epidemiology or public health

prompt_templates.title: Simple criteria generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate inclusion and exclusion criteria for a meta-analysis on the topic: [insert your topic here]
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick criteria generation from a basic topic description

prompt_templates.title: Detailed PICO-based criteria
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the PICO framework, generate inclusion and exclusion criteria for a meta-analysis on: [insert topic]. Specify Population, Intervention, Comparator, and Outcomes for inclusion criteria. Generate exclusion criteria that do not contradict the inclusion criteria.
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured criteria generation with explicit PICO components

prompt_templates.title: Criteria with study design filters
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate inclusion and exclusion criteria for a meta-analysis on: [insert topic]. Include study design restrictions (e.g., RCTs only, cohort studies). Follow the PICO framework and ensure exclusion criteria are consistent with inclusion criteria. Enclose the final criteria list in curly braces for extraction.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced criteria generation with study type filtering

prompt_templates.title: Full protocol-ready criteria set
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete set of eligibility criteria for a systematic review on: [insert topic]. Follow the PICO framework. Include: (1) detailed inclusion criteria with justification, (2) exclusion criteria that are distinct from and consistent with inclusion criteria, (3) any time period or publication status restrictions. Format the final criteria inside curly braces. After generation, run the extraction script to produce the final formatted output.
prompt_templates.scenario: Comprehensive criteria generation for a formal systematic review protocol

output_examples.input: Generate inclusion and exclusion criteria for a meta-analysis on the effectiveness of cognitive behavioral therapy for anxiety disorders.
output_examples.output:
**Inclusion Criteria:**
Participants: Adults aged 18-65 with diagnosed anxiety disorder (GAD, social anxiety, panic disorder)
Interventions: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (individual or group format)
Comparators: Waitlist control, treatment as usual, or active control
Outcomes: Standardized anxiety symptom measures (e.g., BAI, HAM-A, STAI)
Study Design: Randomized controlled trials

**Exclusion Criteria:**
Studies with comorbid psychotic disorders
Pharmacotherapy-only interventions without a psychological component
Case studies, case series, or qualitative studies only
Non-English language publications

output_examples.input: Generate inclusion and exclusion criteria for a meta-analysis on the efficacy of remdesivir for COVID-19 treatment in hospitalized patients.
output_examples.output:
**Inclusion Criteria:**
Participants: Hospitalized adults with confirmed SARS-CoV-2 infection
Interventions: Remdesivir at any approved or investigational dose
Comparators: Placebo, standard of care, or alternative antiviral therapy
Outcomes: Clinical recovery time, mortality at 28 days, adverse events
Study Design: Randomized controlled trials and quasi-experimental studies

**Exclusion Criteria:**
Pediatric populations (under 18 years)
Outpatient or mild cases not requiring hospitalization
Animal model or in vitro studies
Preprint-only publications without peer review

output_examples.input: Generate inclusion and exclusion criteria for a meta-analysis on Mediterranean diet adherence and cardiovascular outcomes.
output_examples.output:
**Inclusion Criteria:**
Participants: Adults (18+) without known cardiovascular disease at baseline
Interventions: Mediterranean diet with or without supplementation
Comparators: Low-fat diet, usual diet, or minimal intervention control
Outcomes: Major adverse cardiovascular events (MI, stroke, CV death), lipid profile changes
Study Design: Randomized controlled trials and prospective cohort studies

**Exclusion Criteria:**
Studies limited to single dietary components (e.g., olive oil only) without overall dietary pattern
Follow-up duration less than 12 months
Studies in populations with established cardiovascular disease at baseline
Non-English publications without available translation

best_practices: Provide specific search terms and keywords for more precise and relevant criteria generation
Review generated criteria against established systematic review standards in your field before final use
Always run the Python extraction script to ensure consistent formatting of the final output
anti_patterns: Providing vague or overly broad topics leads to generic criteria that lack scientific rigor
Skipping the Python extraction script step may result in poorly formatted criteria output
Using the skill without domain literature consultation may produce criteria that miss field-specific standards

faq.question: What is the PICO framework used by this skill?
faq.answer: PICO stands for Population, Intervention, Comparator, and Outcomes. This skill uses PICO to structure inclusion criteria so they cover all key elements of a research question for systematic reviews.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for non-medical meta-analyses?
faq.answer: Yes. The PICO framework can be adapted to social sciences, education, and other fields. Provide clear keywords relevant to your domain for best results.

faq.question: How do I run the Python extraction script?
faq.answer: Run 'python scripts/extract_criteria.py --inclusion "text" --exclusion "text"' with the LLM output texts. The script extracts content from curly braces and formats inclusion and exclusion criteria sections.

faq.question: Will this skill perform statistical meta-analysis calculations?
faq.answer: No. This skill only generates eligibility criteria. It does not perform statistical analysis, effect size calculations, or data synthesis. Use dedicated statistical tools for analysis.

faq.question: Can I customize the generated criteria after creation?
faq.answer: Yes. The generated criteria are a starting template. You should review, refine, and customize them based on your specific research question and field standards.

faq.question: What if the skill cannot generate criteria from my input?
faq.answer: Provide more specific keywords or a clearer research question. If criteria still cannot be generated, the output will state that no criteria were found and suggest improving the input.
